title: Polish: chat tab bar visibility for closed chats and diverged chat title
body:
Polish item tracking the chat tab bar visibility behavior in the Agents window session header.

## Context

Addressed in #323789. Previously the chat tab strip disappeared once only a single open chat remained, hiding closed-but-reopenable chats and any chat whose title had diverged from the session title.

## Behavior after the PR

The tab strip (a single shared observable `IActiveSession.shouldShowChatTabs`) is shown when:
- the session has more than one chat (counting closed, non-tool chats), or
- its single remaining chat's title diverged from the session title.

Chat-to-chat navigation (next/previous chat, the `Ctrl+Tab` switcher) is gated separately on `SessionHasMultipleOpenChatsContext` (more than one *open* tab), so those chords stay a no-op / fall back to session navigation when only a single open chat remains.

## Possible follow-up polish

- [ ] Validate the visual transition when the strip appears/disappears (no layout jump in the header).
- [ ] Confirm the trailing **New Chat** / **Conversations** controls move cleanly between the header and the strip across all the visibility transitions (closed chats, diverged title, drafts).
- [ ] Double-check keyboard navigation precedence (`Ctrl+Tab`, `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+]`/`[`) in the single-open-chat edge cases.
- [ ] Verify behavior with tool-origin (subagent) chats present.

Ref: #323789
